codeReview.summary: The documentation packet (docs/modules/orchestrator.md) is thorough, accurate, and well-grounded in the actual codebase. Every substantive claim was verified against the source: outbox lifecycle statuses match OutboxEvent.Status enum, retry constants match EventPublisherService (MAX_RETRY_ATTEMPTS=5, RETRY_BASE_DELAY_SECONDS=30), config properties match @Value annotations, event bridge phases match @TransactionalEventListener annotations, deprecated seams match actual exceptions thrown, and all 6 Micrometer gauge names match the registered metrics. Cross-references to 7 external docs files all exist. The packet covers all required surfaces: outbox path, listener bridges, schedulers, retries, dead-letter behavior, feature flags, and deprecated/no-op seams. No blocking issues found.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker overwrote an existing garbled draft of orchestrator.md rather than editing it incrementally. This approach (Create tool replacing the entire file) is reasonable for a docs-only rewrite but deviates from the general pattern of incremental edits. AGENTS.md does not explicitly address when full-file replacement is acceptable versus incremental edits.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows: Tool: Create with file_path=docs/modules/orchestrator.md. The previous version was garbled with broken table rows, so a full replacement was justified.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The EventPublisherService uses an AtomicBoolean mutex (publishingInProgress) in addition to ShedLock for preventing concurrent publishing. This dual-locking strategy (in-memory mutex for single-instance + ShedLock for distributed) is not documented in .factory/library/ but was correctly captured in the docs packet. Future workers touching the outbox should know about this dual-layer protection.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: EventPublisherService.java line 87: private final AtomicBoolean publishingInProgress = new AtomicBoolean(false); and line 118: if (!publishingInProgress.compareAndSet(false, true)). The docs packet does not explicitly call out the AtomicBoolean mutex, only ShedLock, but this is a minor omission since the behavior is functionally the same.

summary: PASS. The docs/modules/orchestrator.md packet is a high-quality, code-accurate documentation of the orchestrator and event surfaces. All key claims were verified against source code: outbox lifecycle statuses (PENDING→PUBLISHING→PUBLISHED/FAILED), retry constants (MAX_RETRY_ATTEMPTS=5, RETRY_BASE_DELAY_SECONDS=30 with exponential backoff), config properties and defaults, event bridge phases, scheduler crons, feature flags, and deprecated seam exceptions all match the implementation. All 7 cross-referenced docs files exist. The packet fulfills VAL-PLAT-004 by covering outbox publishing, listener bridges, schedulers, retries, dead-letter behavior, feature flags, and explicitly disclosing 7 deprecated/dead/no-op seams. No blocking issues found. Two minor non-blocking observations: (1) the in-memory AtomicBoolean mutex in EventPublisherService is not explicitly called out in the docs (only ShedLock is mentioned), and (2) the conventions for when full-file replacement is acceptable could be documented.
